title: MediSave
text:
MediSave - SupportGoWhere
MediSave
Share link
A personal healthcare savings account that helps you pay for you and loved ones' healthcare expenses over your lifetime, especially when you retire and no longer have regular income.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Covers selected medical, treatment and hospital expenses, up to the approved limits
Covers for healthcare insurance premiums, rehabilitation and palliative care
Applicable at all MediSave accredited Public Healthcare Institutions (PHIs)
Collapse all sections
About this support

MediSave pays for a variety of medical expenses such as selected outpatient treatments, day surgery and hospitalisation expenses

It also covers the costs for your rehabilitation and palliative care

You can also tap on your MediSave to pay for health insurance premiums for yourself and your loved ones

Who is this for?

You need to be a Singapore Citizen (SC) or Permanent Resident (PR)

Your spouse, children and parents can be of any nationality, except grandparents and siblings who must be SC or PR

How to apply?

To use your MediSave, inform the Public Healthcare Institutions (PHIs) or Medical Institutions (MIs) where you are receiving treatment at, and you would like to use your MediSave to pay for your medical expenses

If this is your first time applying, you will need to sign the Medical Claims Authorisation Form (MCAF)

If you had previously given a standing authorisation for the use of MediSave, the MI will submit a claim on your behalf

The PHI and/or MI will submit the medical claim on your behalf and payment from your healthcare plans will be made directly to them

Upon approval of the claim, the Board and/or insurer will notify you accordingly

Where can I find help?

View more details

Healthcare Hotline: 1800 222 3399

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch
Note: By appointment only

Scheme last updated 23 Sep 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
